cluster: C2 — Honest endings: restart supervisor final frame, loader gate for Exited (?), foreground exit-code propagation, -e/--env self-pollution

play_verdicts.play: FDR play (dossier): restart supervisor writes the final frame (start.c:288-372) + loader stops laundering State.ExitCode into has_exit_code for non-exited records (record.c:658-665), unblocking the honest 'Exited (?)' at list.c:135
play_verdicts.reason: Both findings re-verified against source: run_restart_supervisor ends with _exit(0) at start.c:371 and never marks; the waiter that marks (start.c:1106-1117) is gated off for restart because target_pid_pipe is created only when '!console_mode && !restart_enabled' (start.c:1017). The writer bakes ExitCode 0 into every running record (record.c:97) and the loader trusts it back only via the State-object FALLBACK (record.c:661-665) — the lowercase 'exit_code' key (written only when has_exit_code, record.c:261-262) is the primary path, so the gate is surgical: it only affects records hold wrote while running and foreign records. store and id are in scope at the fork site (the waiter at start.c:1112 and the broker call at 1158-1159 already use them), and hold_mark_run_finished (record.c:972-1033) is pure fd work on absolute store paths, safe after setsid and after apply_child_env mutates the supervisor's environment (store->record_dir is a struct copy, no getenv). No test pins the fabricated 'Exited (0)' — the suite's Exited(N) assertions (test_hold.sh:640,1993,2715,2806,3303,3316) all go through the waiter's real mark. But the sketch has a real defect and two under-specified edges (amendments below), so accept only amended.
amendments: Dossier assumption 3 is wrong as sketched: 'status' is declared INSIDE the while loop (start.c:355); calling hold_mark_run_finished(status) before _exit(0) at 371 reads out-of-scope/uninitialized storage when g_restart_stop fires before the first waitpid completes. Hoist 'int status' with a 'bool have_status' flag; on the stop path with !have_status, skip the mark and let the record render 'Exited (?)' — never record a synthesized or garbage status.

Also mark on the fork-failure exit at start.c:342 (_exit(127)) with status 127<<8 when the record exists; otherwise that death path stays unwitnessed. Do NOT touch the pre-handshake _exit(127) paths (306-325): the parent sees the errno handshake and rolls the launch back before any record is written.

Gate condition in record.c must be: take the State.ExitCode fallback only when the already-parsed state (record.c:652-657) is 'exited' OR has_ended_at (646-651) is set — both parse before line 658 so it is implementable in place. Gating on has_ended_at alone is insufficient reasoning; gating on state alone is fine for the pinned foreign-record fixture (test_docker_shaped_record_fallback_reader, test_hold.sh:2977-3050, Status 'exited' + FinishedAt) but use the OR for belt-and-braces.

Do NOT change the writer: State.ExitCode must keep being emitted (0 while running, exactly like docker) — test_hold.sh:2956-2960 pins the full State key set, and inspect showing ExitCode 0 for running calls is docker's own behavior. Cut the self-deception at the loader only.
Do NOT touch the public-index loader (record.c:~873): test_public_root_index_list_reads_projected_state (test_hold.sh:2664-2698) pins that separate path.

Mirror the waiter's 50x100ms retry loop around the supervisor's mark (record may not exist yet when a fast-failing command exhausts on-failure:0 before the parent's record write at start.c:1408 — identical race the waiter already survives).

Harden test_log_viewer_integrated_chrome_help_and_info (test_hold.sh:955-984): it asserts 'VIEWING EXITED (0)' at :979 after a fixed 'sleep 0.3' at :966. Today a slow waiter is masked by the loader's laundering (interactive_view_exit_code, signal.c:499-511, sees the fabricated 0); with the gate that masking is gone and the fixed sleep becomes a flake. Replace the sleep with the existing record_ended_soon poll (test_hold.sh:519-530).

Accept and document the residual honest hole: 'hold end' on a restart call whose worker ignores TERM escalates to SIGKILL of the whole group after STOP_TIMEOUT_MS (signal.c:280,298); the supervisor dies unmarked and the call renders 'Exited (?)' where docker would say 137. That is the FDR trade working as designed — write it into the new test's comments and never assert an exact code on a forced-kill path.

play_verdicts.play: Foreground bare launch propagates the held process's exit code like docker run (start.c:1497-1517 tail branch), with a docker-parity-contract.md amendment
play_verdicts.reason: Finding re-verified: hold_tail_log_until_exit returns 0 unconditionally on every non-fatal path (signal.c:173-238; error paths die, they never return nonzero), and start.c:1503/1517 uses that as the CLI exit code. No test pins foreground rc for a failing command — every 'exit N' fixture in the suite launches with -d (test_hold.sh:629,2707,2788,3285), and the foreground shape tests (test_hold.sh:3423-3450, test_040_dockerish.sh:37 under set -Eeuo pipefail) run exit-0 commands only, so they pin rc==0 for SUCCESS, which the play preserves. The parity contract currently says only that foreground prints nothing of its own (docker-parity-contract.md:29-30) — the exit code is genuinely unpinned in docs and tests, so this is a licensed contract AMENDMENT, not a silent break. But the sketch under-specifies three semantics that will bite if left to the implementer.
amendments: Scope strictly to the non-console branch (the hold_tail_log_until_exit arm at start.c:1503). Carve off '-it'/console foreground rc parity as named deferral D1: the console path's rc comes from hold_run_native_console, and the broker today never records exit status when ended (broker.c finding, other cluster); propagating an unknowable code there is premature until the broker writes final frames.

Define the algorithm precisely: after tail returns, reload the record (hold_load_record_by_id); if hold_eval_state is STATE_RUNNING (Ctrl-C interrupted the tail; the call stays held) return 0 immediately — never poll; if exited, poll up to 5s (matching the waiter's own 50x100ms retry) for has_exit_code; when marked, return exit_code (wait_status_exit_code already caps at 255, record.c:962-970); when still unmarked after the poll, print one stderr note ('hold: exit status unknown for call <id>') and return 1 — never fabricate 0 for an unwitnessed death, that would rebuild the exact lie this cluster removes.

--rm interplay (start.c:1505-1510): the child's code must win; keep the existing 'prune failure surfaces only when rc==0' shape so 'hold --rm cmd' matches docker run --rm (container code wins over cleanup errors).

Sequence dependency: land AFTER the FDR play. Foreground --restart calls (legal today; only tty is gated, start.c:823-825) anchor the record to the supervisor pid — without the supervisor's final frame the new rc path would hit the 5s-poll-then-rc-1 branch on every policy exhaustion.

Contract redline in the same commit: add to docker-parity-contract.md Flags section: 'A foreground launch exits with the held process's exit status once it ends (docker run parity). Interrupting the tail (Ctrl-C) leaves the call held and exits 0.' Also add 'Exited (?)' to the STATUS honest-extras list at docker-parity-contract.md:56-58 while the file is open — that phrase becomes user-visible via the FDR play and the contract should own it.
Explicitly out of scope (do not balloon): docker's 125/126/127 launch-failure codes — hold returns 1 for command-not-found today (start.c:1226-1239) and no finding licenses changing it.

play_verdicts.play: -e/--env/--env-file become recipe data only: delete the setenv() into hold's own process (cli_main.c:266) before store/invocation resolution
play_verdicts.reason: Finding re-verified as FACT: append_env_assignment setenvs at cli_main.c:266 and --env-file routes through it (cli_main.c:317); the leading-flag loop (cli_main.c:520-527) runs before hold_detect_invocation (693) and store init (745 → getenv("HOME") in store/layout.c). apply_child_env (start.c:415-438) is called on all three spawn paths — restart supervisor (314), console broker's pre-fork (1134, before hold_run_console_broker at 1157), and the plain target (1134 before execv 1191) — so the recorded env array fully replaces the setenv for every child, including HOME. The decisive proof the recipe path is sufficient: REDIAL already applies env exclusively via the recorded recipe (call.c loads recipe.env; no setenv anywhere on that path) and its env replay works today. The only test using -e (test_hold.sh:3225, HOLD_DOCKER_ENV=ok observed in the child's log) passes through apply_child_env and survives. One genuine behavior change needs an explicit decision (amendment 2).
amendments: Delete only the setenv block at cli_main.c:266-268; keep the KEY validation (246-265) and the array append (269-279) untouched. --env-file inherits the fix for free.

PATH edge: today '-e PATH=/custom' leaks into hold_resolve_binary_path (start.c:830 → paths.c:93 getenv("PATH")), so the child binary is currently resolved against the -e PATH. After the fix, resolution uses the invoking PATH while the child still receives -e PATH via apply_child_env. Do NOT rebuild resolution against the recipe env (ballooning); instead add one sentence to docker-parity-contract.md: 'The command is resolved against the invoking environment's PATH; -e/--env-file configure the held process's environment only.' Pin it with a test asserting the child sees the -e value while resolution used the invoker's PATH.

This closes the 'hold -e HOLD_TEST_FAIL_RECORD_WRITE=1' amplification of the unguarded production hooks (start.c:1406/1441) but does NOT fix those hooks — exported env still trips them. That is finding-scoped work for another commit (named deferral D3, #ifdef HOLD_TESTING guards), not a rider on this one.

Leave '-e KEY' (no '=') rejection exactly as is — docker's host-value passthrough is the separate low-severity finding (cli_main.c:246-250); folding it in here widens an intake-hygiene fix into feature work (named deferral D4).

play_verdicts.play: Implicit rider in dossier risks: '--rm with --restart' mutual exclusion (cli_main.c:479, docker rejects the combination)
play_verdicts.reason: Real finding (verified: no exclusion check between cli_main.c:445-478 and 479-483, and start.c validates only restart+tty at 823-825), and it interacts with this cluster (the --rm watcher can prune a restart call whose history the FDR play just made recoverable). But it is a parse-time contract change with its own docker-phrased error and its own test next to test_docker_restart_validation_and_tty_gate (test_hold.sh:3526+), owned by the launch-validation surface, not the endings surface. Bundling it here blurs the commit story. Named deferral D2.
amendments: When picked up: reject at parse time with docker's phrasing ('Conflicting options: --restart and --rm'), rc 5, stderr; pin beside test_hold.sh:3526.

pinned_conflicts.what: Pins 'VIEWING EXITED (0)' in the viewer footer. Passes today even when the waiter's mark is slow because the loader launders State.ExitCode 0 into has_exit_code (record.c:661-665 → signal.c:507). The loader gate removes that masking, making the fixed sleep a flake under load — replace the sleep with the record_ended_soon poll (test_hold.sh:519-530). Behavior when the record IS marked is unchanged (lowercase exit_code key, loader branch record.c:658).

Pins that a hand-planted docker-shaped record (no lowercase keys; State.Status 'exited', ExitCode 7, FinishedAt set) loads without 'skipping corrupt record'. The gate must therefore be conditional (state=='exited' || has_ended_at), never a deletion of the State.ExitCode fallback.
Pins that State always contains ExitCode (and the full docker key set). The writer at record.c:97 must keep emitting ExitCode 0 for running records; the fix is loader-side only.
Pins the separate public-index loader path (record.c:~866-876) and its redacted 'exited' STATUS rendering. The gate must be applied only in the private-record loader at record.c:658-665.

After the supervisor starts marking, the record is REWRITTEN by hold_mark_run_finished at policy exhaustion; the test's inspect assertion '"restart": "on-failure:2"' (:3521) then reads the rewritten record. It survives because writer/loader round-trip the recipe ('restart' written at record.c:311-314, parsed at 776-778; env at 124-128/747-751) — the waiter path already proves this rewrite in production. Also the trailing '"$HOLD_BIN" stop' (:3523) must tolerate an already-exited record — hold_do_signal_action's SIGNAL_TARGET_EXITED path (signal.c:255-261) returns 0, so no change needed; verify in the full run.

pinned_conflicts.where: tests/test_hold.sh:3423-3450 and tests/test_040_dockerish.sh:37 (foreground shape tests under set -Eeuo pipefail)
pinned_conflicts.what: Pin foreground rc==0 for exit-0 commands via '|| return 1' / set -e. They pin the SUCCESS half of exit-code propagation and must keep passing unmodified; no test anywhere pins foreground rc for a failing command, so the propagation is an unpinned gap needing a new pin plus the contract redline.
Assert 'Exited' with no code after 'hold end' of detached non-console calls — waiter-marked (exit_code 143), unaffected by all three plays; listed to show they were checked, not to be changed.

Foreground 'prints nothing of its own' says nothing about rc → the propagation is an amendment, and 'Exited (?)' must be added to the STATUS honest-extras sentence, since tests assert the shapes written there ('Tests assert the shapes written here', line 8-9).

implementation_risks: 1. Uninitialized 'status' in run_restart_supervisor: the variable is loop-local (start.c:355); the g_restart_stop-before-first-worker path reaches _exit(0) with no status ever set. Marking there records garbage. Must hoist with have_status and skip the mark when absent (renders honest 'Exited (?)').

2. Supervisor killed before the mark completes: 'hold end' escalates to SIGKILL of the whole group after STOP_TIMEOUT_MS=5000 (signal.c:280,298); a TERM-ignoring worker turns every end into a KILL that erases the supervisor mid-retry → 'Exited (?)' where docker says 137. By design under FDR, but a user-visible change — never assert exact codes on forced-kill test paths, and expect first-contact confusion ('?' read as data loss); the reboot case already renders 'Stale' (list.c:142-146) so '?' stays rare.

3. Foreground rc path hanging or mis-detaching: after tail, a reload+eval_state must gate the 5s exit-code poll — polling when the call is still RUNNING (Ctrl-C detach) would freeze the terminal for 5s; skipping the poll entirely would race the waiter's ~100ms mark and return rc 1 spuriously. The reload-once/eval/poll-only-if-exited order is load-bearing.

4. Mark-vs-record-write race in the supervisor: a command that exhausts on-failure:0 instantly can attempt the mark before the parent's hold_write_record_atomic (start.c:1408). The waiter has the identical race and survives via the 50x100ms retry; the supervisor must copy that idiom exactly, not a single-shot call.

5. -e PATH resolution regression: '-e PATH=/x cmd' currently finds /x/cmd via hold's polluted PATH (paths.c:93); after the fix it resolves against the invoker's PATH. Unpinned, but a silent behavior change — must be documented in the parity contract in the same commit, or a user bisecting will call it a break.

6. Cross-cluster surface collision: the console/broker cluster will also touch start.c (console branch, target_pid_pipe for console mode) and write final frames from broker_cleanup_and_exit. The FDR loader gate composes cleanly (marked broker records use the lowercase exit_code key), but coordinate start.c hunks — the restart-supervisor signature change (1070-1084) and any console-branch pid-anchoring change are adjacent in the same fork site.

7. Supervisor stderr noise lands in the call log: 'hold: restarting ... after exit status N' and any mark-failure message go through the dup2'd stderr pipe into the captured log (start.c:1062). A mark-retry-failure message would pollute user logs — keep the failure path silent or single-line.

8. Test-suite timing discipline: every new assertion must poll (record_ended_soon idiom, test_hold.sh:519-530) — the suite runs under a 25s per-test timeout (HOLD_TEST_TIMEOUT) with per-test isolated HOME (run_test/new_env, test_hold.sh:387-427); fixed sleeps are the existing flake vector the gate newly exposes (risk 1 in pinned conflicts).

9. macOS parity: no new platform seams — hold_mark_run_finished is POSIX fd work already exercised from an equally-detached waiter; the only conditional code near the touched sites is the pipe2/O_CLOEXEC fallback (start.c:960-1037) which is untouched. clear_process_environment's non-glibc branch (start.c:389-413) already runs on macOS for the supervisor path today.
revised_change_set:
Three commits on tempo-refinement, in this order. Baseline gate before and after each: make (zero warnings), bash tests/test_hold.sh (135 pass + new), bash tests/test_040_dockerish.sh, scripts/lint_layers.sh.

COMMIT 1 — "env flags are recipe data, not hold's own environment" (intake hygiene; independent, smallest, lands first).
File: src/cli_main.c. In append_env_assignment (cli_main.c:246-280): delete the setenv block (lines 266-268) and its hold_die_errno; keep key validation and the env-array append. --env-file (294-330) inherits the fix. No other source change — apply_child_env (start.c:415-438) already applies the recorded array on all three spawn paths (supervisor start.c:314, console pre-broker 1134, plain target 1134), and redial proves recipe-only env works today.
File: docs/docker-parity-contract.md — one sentence under the -e/--env-file flag entries: the command is resolved against the invoking environment's PATH; -e configures the held process's environment only.
Tests: new test_env_flag_is_recipe_data_only (see test plan).

COMMIT 2 — "honest final frames: supervisor writes its exit status; unwitnessed deaths render Exited (?)" (the FDR play; one commit because supervisor-fix and loader-gate together land restart calls on the true 'Exited (N)' with no intermediate half-state).
File: src/runtime/start.c. (a) Extend run_restart_supervisor's signature with 'const struct hold_store *store, const char *id' (static function; both args in scope at the call site 1070-1084). (b) Hoist 'int status' out of the while loop with 'bool have_status=false'; set have_status=true after each successful waitpid. (c) Before _exit(0) at line 371: if have_status, mark via a retry loop mirroring the waiter (start.c:1111-1117, 50x100ms hold_mark_run_finished(store,id,status)); if !have_status, skip (honest unknown). (d) On the fork-failure path at line 342: attempt the same mark with status 127<<8 before _exit(127). (e) Leave the pre-handshake _exit(127) paths (306-327) untouched — the parent rolls back before any record exists.
File: src/store/record.c. At the loader's State.ExitCode fallback (661-665): take it only when (r->has_state && strcmp(r->state,"exited")==0) || r->has_ended_at — both fields are parsed above (652-657, 646-651). Do not touch the lowercase exit_code branch (658-660), the writer (97 keeps emitting ExitCode 0 while running — pinned key set), or the public-index loader (~873).
File: docs/docker-parity-contract.md — add 'Exited (?)' to the honest-extras STATUS sentence (56-58).
Tests: new test_restart_final_status_recorded, test_end_restart_call_records_term_status, test_unwitnessed_death_renders_exited_unknown; harden test_log_viewer_integrated_chrome_help_and_info (:966 sleep → record_ended_soon poll). Register with run_test next to the existing restart tests (:4123-4124).

COMMIT 3 — "foreground bare launch exits with the held process's status" (contract amendment; depends on commit 2 for foreground --restart correctness).
File: src/runtime/start.c, tail branch only (1497-1517), non-console arm (the hold_tail_log_until_exit call at 1503): after tail returns 0, reload the record by id; if hold_eval_state==STATE_RUNNING return 0 (interrupted tail, call stays held); if exited, poll <=5s (50x100ms) reloading until has_exit_code; on marked return r.exit_code; on timeout print 'hold: exit status unknown for call <short-id>' to stderr and return 1. Preserve the --rm shape: child's rc wins; prune failure replaces rc only when rc==0 (mirror existing 1505-1510 logic). Do not touch the console arm (hold_run_native_console) — deferral D1.
File: docs/docker-parity-contract.md — amend the foreground paragraph (29-30): foreground exits with the held process's exit status; Ctrl-C leaves the call held and exits 0.
Tests: new test_docker_foreground_propagates_exit_code (plus --rm variant inside it); existing foreground shape tests (3423-3450, test_040:37) stay green as the success-case pin.

NAMED DEFERRALS (do not fold into this cluster): D1 console/-it foreground rc parity → console/broker cluster (broker must write final frames first; broker.c:293 finding). D2 --rm x --restart parse-time rejection (cli_main.c:479) → launch-validation commit beside test_docker_restart_validation_and_tty_gate. D3 #ifdef HOLD_TESTING guards for start.c:1406/1441 production hooks. D4 '-e KEY' host-value passthrough (cli_main.c:246-250). D5 docker's 'Restarting (N)' STATUS phrase for a supervised-down worker. D6 inspect still reporting stored 'running' for unwitnessed deaths — list is the contract surface this cluster fixes; inspect staleness is a separate feedback finding.
test_plan: NEW test_restart_final_status_recorded (register via run_test beside :4123): out=$("$HOLD_BIN" -d --restart on-failure:1 --restart-delay 0 -- /bin/sh -c 'exit 3' 2>&1) || return 1; id=$(printf '%s\n' "$out" | extract_id); record_ended_soon "$id" || return 1; rec=$(record_path "$id"); grep -q '"state": "exited"' "$rec" && grep -q '"exit_code": 3' "$rec" || { cat "$rec" >&2; return 1; }; "$HOLD_BIN" list -a | grep -Eq "^${id}[[:space:]].*Exited \\(3\\)" || return 1; inspect State.Status==exited and ExitCode==3 via the suite's python3 heredoc idiom.

NEW test_end_restart_call_records_term_status: start "$HOLD_BIN" -d --restart always --restart-delay 0 -- /bin/sleep 300; "$HOLD_BIN" end "$id" >/dev/null; record_ended_soon "$id"; list -a matches "Exited \\(143\\)". Comment in-test: a TERM-ignoring worker forces KILL escalation and legitimately renders 'Exited (?)' — never assert an exact code on a forced-kill variant.

NEW test_unwitnessed_death_renders_exited_unknown: live variant — out=$("$HOLD_BIN" -d -- /bin/sh -c 'kill -9 "$PPID"; exit 5' 2>&1) kills the waiter so nobody marks; poll list -a (60x0.05s) until "^${id}.*Exited"; then assert the row matches 'Exited \\(\\?\\)' and NOT 'Exited \\(0\\)'. Deterministic fallback if the PPID timing flakes in CI: plant a docker-shaped record (copy the :2989 fixture) with State.Status "running", dead Pid/Pgid/Sid (e.g. 999999), no FinishedAt, no lowercase exit_code; assert list -a shows 'Exited (?)'.

NEW test_docker_foreground_propagates_exit_code: set +e; "$HOLD_BIN" -- /bin/sh -c 'exit 7'; rc=$?; set -e; [ "$rc" -eq 7 ]; "$HOLD_BIN" -- /bin/true || return 1 (success stays 0); set +e; "$HOLD_BIN" --rm -- /bin/sh -c 'exit 3'; rc=$?; set -e; [ "$rc" -eq 3 ] and path_absent_soon on the record/log (reuse :556 helper).

NEW test_env_flag_is_recipe_data_only: fake="$TEST_ROOT/fake-home"; out=$("$HOLD_BIN" -e HOME="$fake" -d -- /bin/sh -c 'echo "home:$HOME"; sleep 0.1' 2>&1); id=$(extract_id); record_path "$id" "$HOME/.local/state/hold" must exist; [ ! -e "$fake/.local/state/hold" ]; poll logs --plain for 'home:'"$fake" (child received it via apply_child_env). Add a PATH sub-assertion: "$HOLD_BIN" -e PATH=/nonexistent -d -- sleep 0.1 still resolves sleep (invoker PATH used for resolution).

UPDATE tests/test_hold.sh:966 (test_log_viewer_integrated_chrome_help_and_info): replace the fixed 'sleep 0.3' with 'record_ended_soon "$id" || return 1' so the pinned 'VIEWING EXITED (0)' at :979 no longer depends on the loader laundering a slow waiter's gap.

VERIFY-ONLY (no edits expected, watch in the full run): test_docker_restart_policy_restarts_failures (:3501) — inspect's '"restart"' assertion now reads a supervisor-rewritten record; test_docker_shaped_record_fallback_reader (:2977) — foreign exited record still loads through the gated fallback; test_public_root_index_list_reads_projected_state (:2664) — public loader untouched; test_lifecycle (:566) and every 'Exited \\(N\\)' assertion (:640, :1993, :2715, :2806, :3303, :3316) — all waiter-marked, must stay green.

FULL GATES per commit: make (zero warnings, -std=c11 -Wall -Wextra -Wpedantic), bash tests/test_hold.sh (all pass, no new SKIPs), bash tests/test_040_dockerish.sh, scripts/lint_layers.sh; grep -rn 'Exited (0)' docs/ to confirm no doc example now shows a fabricated success.
